finding_results.notes: The subreddit, Quora, and later search results are now framed as Nate's shaky internet takeaway rather than narrator-certified telecom procedure. The revision names the live unknowns: whether Martin's carrier matches the examples Nate is reading, whether deleted audio survives or only a call record does, and whether anything remains recoverable at all.

finding_results.notes: After the voicemail-content/deletion/shame payload, the chapter now makes one short escalation: Nate seeks signal and a cleaner answer, gets only unresolved uncertainty, and Mallick arrives immediately after. The duplicated deck/courtroom true-crime re-certification beat was removed.
The draft board remains present in the room, but the repeated red-marker description is gone.
The third-step motif is reduced to a simple action beat with no repeated explanation or duration summary.

Three abstract, essay-register phrases replaced with concrete, pressured, character-true speech. (1) 'Commentary on the medium' replaced with 'Like, your mom leaves voicemails. Bill collectors. That's it.' — concrete examples in Nate's list-building mode, followed by 'that's the joke, that's the whole —' with an unfinished restart. (2) 'the language — ... the language we use' replaced with 'the things we say — ... the shit we say to each other' — colloquial, profanity-true for Nate's moderate-high profile under pressure, removes the abstractly analytical framing. (3) 'That's the dialect' replaced with 'Every guy in every league says that stuff' — appeals to concrete shared behavior instead of labeling it with a sociolinguistic term. All three fixes preserve the surrounding pressure cadence, the accidental first-person slip at 'when I said,' and the full confession arc through the deletion admission.

Recast the flagged descriptive beats into varied sentence shapes and image sources, preserving the same scene function and comic pressure while removing the repeated person-template comparison scaffold.

pass_summaries.summary: Resolved all four packet findings in chapter_06. The voicemail-recovery material now reads as Nate's frightened, source-shaky inference with explicit uncertainty; the pre-interview run-up is compressed to a single unresolved-search beat before Mallick appears; and the repeated red-marker and third-step explanations are removed. Nate's voicemail-deletion timing was also aligned to the continuity sheet's roughly 3:15 AM value.

Single finding addressed across three surgical edits targeting Nate's confession lead-in (lines 135, 139, 153). Removed two essay-register phrases ('Commentary on the medium,' 'That's the dialect') and one abstractly analytical framing ('the language we use') that momentarily made Nate sound like a media critic diagnosing his own speech. Replacements use concrete examples, list-building, mild profanity, and unfinished restarts consistent with Nate's style-bible voice under interrogation pressure. The confession arc, accidental self-incrimination, and all chapter hooks are preserved intact.

Revised chapter 6 in place to eliminate the repeated simile/template chassis identified by the packet while preserving Nate's panic spiral, the voicemail reveal, and Mallick's folksy interview pressure.
